Spotlight: Japanese horse who is a two-time Grade 1 scorer on home soil and has finished a creditable second in the last two runnings of the 6f Group 1 at Royal Ascot, beaten only a nose by Almeraq the latest time; could go one better at Newmarket, as was the case with compatriot Agnes World who was runner-up at Royal Ascot prior to winning this contest in 2000.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld, Dubai July Cup Stakes (Group 1)
Spotlight: Very useful 3yo but finished only eighth, albeit best of the near-side runners, in the Commonwealth Cup for 3yos at Royal Ascot when bidding to follow up his trial success over the same C&D; bare form of the latest effort gives him work to do with Venetian Sun and Division; furthermore, it took his Group 1 record to 0-3.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld, Dubai July Cup Stakes (Group 1)
Spotlight: Solid performer who completed a hat-trick during his 2yo campaign and has strong placed form this season, the last two times behind Venetian Sun; switched and finished well for a close third in the Commonwealth Cup at Royal Ascot latest; has broadly progressive RPRs in tandem with a gradual rise in class; knocking on the door at Group level.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld, Dubai July Cup Stakes (Group 1)
Spotlight: Progressive 3yo gelding who brings impressive stats, being 3-3 (all over 5f against his elders) this term and 3-3 over 6f; finished strongly to get up in the final strides in the 5f Group 1 at Royal Ascot (good to firm) most recently; all bar one of his trainer's five July Cup winners have been 3yos; big player back up in distance.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld, Dubai July Cup Stakes (Group 1)
Spotlight: 3yo filly who is unbeaten (6-6) over sprint trips, mostly in Group races, including two at the top level; only narrowly justified favouritism in the Commonwealth Cup at Royal Ascot (good to firm) most recently but it's hard to knock her record and there may yet be further advancement in terms of her rating; sire won the July Cup; receives all the allowances and duly warrants respect.
Yet to add to tally since maiden win at Tipperary last year; good third at Leopardstown in May (1m2f, good) but hasn't built on that in two runs since; needs to get back on track.

Gained a fourth win of her career at Dundalk (1m4f) in March; has won twice on turf and not beaten far at Gowran on penultimate (1m4f, yielding); nudged up 3lb for that and has never won off a mark this high; 1m2f may be on sharp side these days.

Handicap debutant went close in a 10.5f maiden at Dundalk in May; soft ground may not have suited on latest; wears blinkers for the first time and could go well for yard with decent record at this venue.

Solid strike rate on the AW (4-8) but remains a maiden on turf after seven starts; however, ran an excellent race to finish a 4l second of 12 in this headgear at the Curragh last time (1m2f, gd-fm); enters calculations off the same mark here.

Five-time winner (two on turf) effective at 1m-1m4f; had been running well on the AW this year before a poor effort on testing ground at the Curragh when last seen in April; this ground more suitable and has gone well fresh, but yet to win off this high a mark.

Won a 1m handicap at Gowran in May off 14lb lower and added two 10.5f handicaps at Dundalk in the autumn; not a bad run on seasonal return at Gowran last month, but remains 6lb above her last win so new headgear combination must eke out more.
AW winner for Charlie Johnston last year; has failed to fire on all three starts for this yard; mark sliding but can only be watched for now.

Won off this mark at Down Royal (10.5f, good) for Henry De Bromhead in June 2025; well below best on last two starts for Adrian Nicholls and has moved yards again; likely best watched after absence unless the market speaks.
Dual-purpose; bolted up in Sligo maiden hurdle last month (2m2f, good); previously soundly beaten over this C&D (gd-yld), extending his Flat record to 0-11; 3lb lower now but others appeal more.

Debut 6f maiden win at Sligo last August remains this 3yo's sole success; soundly beaten all three starts this season at 6.5f-1m on varying ground; hopes pinned on this longer trip sparking something (dam won at 1m2f-1m6f).
Didn't show much in three maiden runs last season; similar story on seasonal return and handicap debut at Roscommon in May; has moved to a new yard; 5lb wrong and likely best watched.

1m2f winner for the Kublers last season and struck at the second time of asking for her new stable in 1m2f Newbury handicap (good to firm) in June, edging out a next-time-out winner with the pair clear; raised 7lb and needs to prove her effectiveness at a shorter trip now.

Low-mileage 4yo who reappeared with a creditable third of 11 in 1m Yarmouth handicap (soft; showed promise on good last year) in June, shaping as if the run would bring her forward; unlikely she's reached her limit.

Won Salisbury novice (7f, good to firm) and Epsom handicap (extended 1m, good to soft) last year; only seventh of 14 on 1m Kempton reappearance but the fact she went off 15-8 favourite suggests better was anticipated.
Came good in a small-field novice at Leicester (1m, good to firm) in May but finished well behind a couple of these in the Sandringham here since; others make more appeal.
0-5 since debut win but made a good start to her handicap career when fourth (Rosa Inglesa third) in Sandringham over C&D at the Royal meeting (good to firm); claims if she can build on that.

First attempt at around 1m when winning handicap at Nottingham (good to soft) in May and backed it up with a very good third (Blingy's Sister fourth) of 30 in the Sandringham over C&D (good to firm) 22 days ago, impressing with the move she made from off the pace to hit the front in the penultimate furlong; another prominent showing is on the cards.
Has started her handicap career with solid placed efforts at Newmarket (7f, good to firm) and Thirsk (1m, good); likely to go well again
Had plenty of chances from marks in the mid 70s last season, ending it with two below-par runs; her mark has eased as a result but it's not enough to tempt back from nine months off.

Turned over at odds on on first two starts this season but made no mistake with cheekpieces added (retained) in maiden at Yarmouth (1m, good to firm) 23 days ago, making all; not obviously progressive but still no surprise were she to kick on now she's got her head in front.

Prescott filly who ended her 2yo campaign by making all in 1m Newcastle novice and returned to win a Salisbury handicap over the same trip (good to firm) 11 days ago; a 4lb rise may not stop her with further progress looking assured.

Progressed very well last term, culminating in his close third in Group 2 Great Voltigeur here (1m4f, good to firm) in August, until not so good trying 1m6f on final outing; rallied when close second in 1m4f French Listed event on reappearance but needs to bounce back from a tame show in Newbury Group 3; tongue tied first time on this second attempt at 1m6f.

OwnerName: A Cope, B Roberts And J Biggane
Spotlight: Eight AW wins, two last winter in 2m handicaps; however, he failed to beat a rival in Group 3 at Sandown (2m, good) on turf return, has tons to prove on turf record (0-5) and even best AW form does not look good enough.

7yo who is 0-12 since winning at Meydan in January 2025; has run really well in plenty of Group races in the interim, including the last two runnings of the Yorkshire Cup over C&D, but he ran a stinker in C&D Listed event four weeks ago; bang there if he bounces straight back.
5yo who is an improved performer this season and registered career-best form when a close third in C&D Listed race last time, a neck behind Tabletalk; should be bang there once again.

Won the Melrose Handicap over C&D in August 2024; 0-9 since but he was runner-up in five of those races, including when beaten a neck in C&D Listed event four weeks ago, narrowly in front of Mount Atlas; the lack of a recent win is a touch frustrating but he's clearly a leading contender.

Won at Hexham last August in the first of her three bumpers but a Grade 2 at Aintree (125-1) proved far too tough in April; will build at some point on the modest form shown in a 9.4f maiden on her recent first venture under Flat rules, but she's impossible to fancy today.

RaceName: Minuty, The Spirit Of Saint-Tropez Handicap
Spotlight: Drops back in trip/grade having never landed a blow in the Northumberland Vase last time; broadly consistent otherwise this term and won at Musselburgh (12.5f) in April; 4lb higher now, though.

RaceName: Minuty, The Spirit Of Saint-Tropez Handicap
Spotlight: Seems in form having posted a couple of creditable efforts over fences this year (May/June); close second over C&D when last seen on the Flat ten months ago; capable of taking a serious hand.

RaceName: Minuty, The Spirit Of Saint-Tropez Handicap
Spotlight: Well suited by about 1m4f; form over C&D features a success (off current mark) in this race last year and resurgent effort, doing well to finish third having been slowly away then come from a difficult position, in latest start; respected.

RaceName: Minuty, The Spirit Of Saint-Tropez Handicap
Spotlight: Finished last of six at Ripon on stable/seasonal debut but is interesting assuming he's back in better form with the outing under his belt; dual winner off higher marks at Chester; check the betting,

RaceName: Minuty, The Spirit Of Saint-Tropez Handicap
Spotlight: Registered his last win off 1lb higher at Windsor a year ago; consistent since, mostly making the frame; successful in two of his last three attempts in Class 5; solid claims back down in grade.

RaceName: Minuty, The Spirit Of Saint-Tropez Handicap
Spotlight: Productive on Flat and over hurdles since early last summer; creditable second, one place in front of L'Eagle Aid who admittedly came from a poorer position, over C&D most recently; major contender.

RaceName: Minuty, The Spirit Of Saint-Tropez Handicap
Spotlight: Has a much better strike-rate on AW than on grass; soundly beaten off his lower turf mark returned to this sphere last time; opposed.

RaceName: Minuty, The Spirit Of Saint-Tropez Handicap
Spotlight: Has a modest strike-rate for current yard but is technically unexposed over today's trip; creditable second at Musselburgh (12.5f) in May in sole attempt beyond 1m2f; could go well off bottom weight.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld, Dubai Apprentice Handicap (Whips to be carried but not used) (Racing Excellence)
Spotlight: Can miss the break and is usually ridden from off the pace; won a Brighton handicap (5.3f) by a wide margin on Tuesday for Jude Fernandes; that made it three wins from his last four starts and he is a leading player under his penalty.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld, Dubai Apprentice Handicap (Whips to be carried but not used) (Racing Excellence)
Spotlight: Plenty of near-misses before finally off the mark over 5f at Bath four weeks ago; the cheekpieces have had a positive effect and the extra furlong looks fine; upped 5lb; needs considering.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld, Dubai Apprentice Handicap (Whips to be carried but not used) (Racing Excellence)
Spotlight: Polytrack winner in a first-time tongue-tie last backend when returning from wind surgery and four months off; that was not the first time she'd gone well fresh and this six-month layoff is not offputting, but she was held at short prices in two runs off her revised mark.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld, Dubai Apprentice Handicap (Whips to be carried but not used) (Racing Excellence)
Spotlight: 2-2 over C&D (both in 2023) and tends to come to hand around this time of year; back on a fair mark and offered more last time, so there are positives for a yard that took this three years ago.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld, Dubai Apprentice Handicap (Whips to be carried but not used) (Racing Excellence)
Spotlight: Sole win came over a stiff 6f and she'll be happier back up in trip having lacked early toe at Windsor last time; yard won this last year, she's down to a career-low mark and sports first-time headgear under 5lb claimer Charlie Tucker; reasons for optimism.

RaceName: Al Basti Equiworld, Dubai Apprentice Handicap (Whips to be carried but not used) (Racing Excellence)
Spotlight: Rare Flat runner for his jumps-orientated yard; his win for Stan Moore came over 1m (Polytrack) and he went 1m2f once, so he has a bit to overcome running after such a long absence and in a sprint for the first time.

Arrives in good form having won at Goodwood (7f, good) then made the frame in competitive field at Epsom (7f, good to soft); however, he remains on a career-high mark and has to concede weight all round.
Ex-French gelding who has raced mainly at about 1m; ran with some promise at Newmarket on British/seasonal debut in June and may build on that effort, provided he copes with the return to 7f.
Has shown major promise (won then good third) in two starts over 7f on AW, latest in January; had wind surgery since; interesting contender, being such an unexposed sort.

Belied his 80-1 odds with close third at York last time (7f, good; second start for new yard) but it remains to be seen whether he can back up that effort; has proved quirky and developed inconsistency.
Gained last three wins on Newcastle AW; ran respectably back there on latest start; has bits of encouraging turf form this term but it's over two years since his last success in this sphere.

Has performed well in two runs at the July festival, notably a win (off just 1lb lower) in this race last year; narrowly justified favouritism at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) latest start, setting up him well for a return to this track; respected.
Successful over C&D on 2yo debut; 0-13 since but ran encouragingly at Windsor (6f) most recently, after a spell at Meydan; returns to this venue with a fighting chance off current mark.
Comfortably held over 1m at Royal Ascot last time; 2-2 in 7f handicaps, both Class 4 events from the front, including over C&D; may have more to offer back at this trip and back in calmer waters.
Record of 2-4, all over 6f, for previous yard; may still do better but has something to prove kept to 7f, having shown a dip in his form when upped to this trip at Doncaster for new stable last time.

Low-mileage 3yo; failed to get home over 1m at Carlisle last time when bidding for a hat-trick; gained her wins over 6f/7f (AW/turf) and remains open to further progress back down in distance; still of interest.

OwnerName: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum
Spotlight: Thrice-raced gelding who showed his strongest form on debut at Doncaster (7f) when beaten only half a length by a colt who currently has a mark of 102; open to progress in handicaps and looks interesting off an opening mark of 77, receiving weight all round.

Won over 1m2f on the AW in March wearing first-time blinkers and easily her best run since when a staying-on third over 1m3f at Naas (good); well held from wide draw at Bellewstown latest but not one to rule out.
Dual-purpose mare hasn't won on the Flat since October 2024; has had a break since two runs on heavy ground at the Curragh in March, may just need this.

Gained fourth career win with 22-1 success over this trip at Cork in May in testing conditions; not so good twice since on faster ground, although 1m1f on quick ground on the short side last time; more needed.
Has won on AW; 0-13 on turf, best recent effort when close third here over 1m5f last month; tame Bellewstown effort since so needs to bounce back.
Starts have hindered his chances so far for this yard, most recently far too free at Bellewstown; handicapper relenting (down 12lb this season) but hard to fancy.
Six-time AW winner but 0-18 on turf, hard to fancy on recent evidence (over further) despite reduced mark although return to 1m2f to suit.

C&D winner two years ago and couple of hurdle wins since, including at last year's Galway festival; excellent comeback run when second at Leopardstown in April (1m), most recently chased home an easy winner over this trip at Down Royal last month on soft; one to consider.
Got off the mark at Gowran (9.5f, good) last summer, two runs this term well below best; much more needed now, does handle fast ground.
Hasn't counted in two handicap runs of late; blinkers now tried and still has time on her side.
Form to date suggests he's better on AW; couple of modest efforts on turf last month so lot more needed.
Best form over 7f/1m, including sole win in Britain as 3yo; tailed off on yard debut at Sligo in May and has had a break since.
Showed a bit of promise last year at Gowran (1m, good), didn't count at same venue on last month's return to action; back up in trip here but hard to fancy.

RaceName: Thames Hospice Trunks Across The Thames Handicap
Spotlight: Best form (and both wins) on the AW; bled from nose back on turf when only eighth of 11 at Nottingham two months ago; needs to bounce back after a break.

RaceName: Thames Hospice Trunks Across The Thames Handicap
Spotlight: All three wins on AW but below par at Kempton last time; fifth of ten at Epsom on sole turf outing last spring; bit to prove back on grass.

RaceName: Thames Hospice Trunks Across The Thames Handicap
Spotlight: All three wins at around 7f on good or good to firm ground; possibly unsuited by 1m on soft at Goodwood last time; 7lb below last winning mark and could be dangerous under optimum conditions.

RaceName: Thames Hospice Trunks Across The Thames Handicap
Spotlight: Won two from five last season and made an encouraging reappearance/stable debut when fourth of nine at Yarmouth (1m, good to firm) three weeks ago; stepping back down to 7f looks a positive move and the booking of promising claimer Jake Dickson is a plus; high on the list.

RaceName: Thames Hospice Trunks Across The Thames Handicap
Spotlight: Won over this trip at Newmarket last autumn for Mick Appleby but three turf runs for current yard have been well below par; bit to prove.

RaceName: Thames Hospice Trunks Across The Thames Handicap
Spotlight: Has a better strike-rate on AW than turf but she wasn't disgraced after a break at Carlisle (1m, good to firm; 40-1) 17 days ago; may come on for that outing and she's not ruled out.

RaceName: Thames Hospice Trunks Across The Thames Handicap
OwnerName: Kevin Frost Racing Club & Mike Humphries
Spotlight: Admirable teenager with 12 wins to his name, including one over C&D; best recent effort when second of 12 at Newbury (7f, good to firm) last month; should be on the premises again.

RaceName: Thames Hospice Trunks Across The Thames Handicap
Spotlight: Arrives in top form after a 7f win at Kempton and two second places over 6f; hard to knock and should be thereabouts.

RaceName: Thames Hospice Trunks Across The Thames Handicap
Spotlight: 7f winner on the AW early in the year but seems to have lost his form and finished last at Yarmouth two weeks ago; fair bit to prove.

RaceName: Thames Hospice Trunks Across The Thames Handicap
Spotlight: On an advancing losing run but he's well handicapped and bubbling under; too free (not for the first time) for a 7lb claimer last time and far from disgraced to finish fourth of eight at Yarmouth; interesting with Cieren Fallon taking over.

RaceName: Thames Hospice Trunks Across The Thames Handicap
Spotlight: Just one win since the first half of 2023; might have needed the run when sixth of 11 at Chester (16-1) on reappearance but he'll have to have come on a good deal to make an impact here.

RaceName: Thames Hospice Trunks Across The Thames Handicap
Spotlight: Returned to form in first-time cheekpieces when winning at Chelmsford last October; unplaced all four starts since and now returns from five months off.

RaceName: Thames Hospice Trunks Across The Thames Handicap
Spotlight: 40-1 winner of novice at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) on reappearance; down the field on handicap debut at York four weeks later (subsequently gelded); has to prove he's as effective on turf as the AW but yard won this with a lightly raced 3yo in 2024.

RaceName: Thames Hospice Trunks Across The Thames Handicap
Spotlight: Dual winner from the front last summer but has shown very little this season; tumbling in the weights but carries clear risks.

RaceName: Thames Hospice Trunks Across The Thames Handicap
OwnerName: Al Shaqab Racing, Miller And Day
Spotlight: Has gone the wrong way since making the frame in novices at Windsor (1m) and Newbury (7f) in the spring; may come good at some point but the handicapper is dropping him only gradually.

RaceName: Thames Hospice Trunks Across The Thames Handicap
Spotlight: 8lb below his last winning mark and ran respectably when third at Newbury 18 days ago, finishing one place behind Documenting; more appealing than some.

RaceName: Thames Hospice Trunks Across The Thames Handicap
Spotlight: Unplaced all four starts but he shaped a bit better than the bare result when sixth of ten on handicap debut at Windsor (6f, good to soft; stayed on after being short of room) five weeks ago; hard to pin down and may have untapped potential.

RaceName: Thames Hospice Trunks Across The Thames Handicap
Spotlight: Most recent win came over this trip at Chepstow in May on final run for Thomas Faulkner; not disgraced over 6f twice since and looks ready for a return to 7f; gets that here and should be thereabouts.

RaceName: Party Time And Limerick Motor Centre Rated Race
Spotlight: Course 7f maiden winner last season on his sixth and final start for Aidan O'Brien; went close in a 6f handicap at Navan on his penultimate start; should be capable of holding his own in this company.

RaceName: Party Time And Limerick Motor Centre Rated Race
OwnerName: F J Carragher/Yorkavenue Syndicate/Park Ave Syndicate
Spotlight: Only one win from 26 starts and that was on AW for Brian Meehan in 2023; fair AW form early this year; hard to fancy on recent form

RaceName: Party Time And Limerick Motor Centre Rated Race
Spotlight: Best of three runs last year when a long-priced third over this trip at Down Royal; lack of an outing this season is a potential negative

RaceName: Party Time And Limerick Motor Centre Rated Race
Spotlight: Much stronger record on AW (5-30, placed nine times) as opposed to turf (1-25, placed four times); two of his last three starts on grass have been satisfactory; probably superior to stablemate Apache Outlaw.

RaceName: Party Time And Limerick Motor Centre Rated Race
Spotlight: Only one win from 16 starts; placed seven times, including a pleasing return to form when second in a 7f handicap at Leopardstown last month; good prospects of reaching the money again.

RaceName: Party Time And Limerick Motor Centre Rated Race
Spotlight: Solid form in three runs on turf since an unproductive spell on AW; 7lb claimer in the saddle here, with a senior rider on stablemate Clonmacash.

RaceName: Party Time And Limerick Motor Centre Rated Race
Spotlight: 7f winner at Gowran at two and a fair fifth in a 7f Listed contest at Fairyhouse, achieving a peak official rating of 90; regressive this term, resulting in a slump to a mark of 79; stable is in flying form; hard to know if he can recapture his best form.

RaceName: Party Time And Limerick Motor Centre Rated Race
Spotlight: Well held in third over a longer trip at Ballinrobe last time, his first time to make the frame; might find this distance inadequate.

RaceName: Party Time And Limerick Motor Centre Rated Race
Spotlight: Half-sister to Group 2 winner Dancing Gemini; mixed form last season, beaten favourite twice; more like it when landing a 7f Dundalk conditions race in February with cheekpieces on; headgear left off now, but makes plenty of appeal in a race of this standard.

RaceName: Party Time And Limerick Motor Centre Rated Race
Spotlight: Sole win came in a 7f Brighton maiden in 2024; nothing of note in his form since finishing third of seven in this race last year; mid-division at the Curragh last time.

RaceName: Party Time And Limerick Motor Centre Rated Race
OwnerName: R Scahill, S Begley, J & C Bourke
Spotlight: Best maiden run was over 7f at Cork on ground verging on heavy; however, he has shown fair form on good ground too; prominent for most of the way before fading into sixth on handicap debut over 1m at Leopardstown.

Rejuvenated with James Owen, reeling off an AW hat-trick in January and making it four on the bounce when scoring at Catterick (6f, good) in May; only just missed out at Doncaster last month and it was 5f when beaten favourite at Sandown last time; now 3lb higher but she did look promising early in her career and Mason Paetel's claim helps too.
Arrives on the crest of a wave having won four of her last five starts; a 4lb rise for her small-field Class 5 win at Ripon last month could just find her out here, though.
Good run of form ground to a halt at Thirsk ten days ago when he wasn't on his best behaviour in the stalls; has the ability to feature off this mark but is not without risk.
Ran his best race for new connections when third of 21 in a 0-85 over C&D four weeks ago; mark left unchanged but he does need to prove himself on ground firmer than good.
Ended 2025 in good form on AW; some solid efforts back on turf this year without suggesting he had much in hand of his mark; minor money perhaps his best hope.

Not the force of old but that is reflected in his handicap mark; has undergone wind surgery since his latest run but it isn't enough to earn him the vote in this competitive race; usual headgear is absent today.

OwnerName: Alfa Site Services & Alan Gove Racing
Spotlight: Made a winning handicap debut (6f, AW) in March and a perfect start to her turf career when making all at Pontefract (6f, good) in June; Doncaster third on latest outing was another good run and this track should prove more to her liking; still capable of better.
Both wins have come on good to soft ground but he does have one good run on good to firm to his name; disappointed at Windsor last month but this is an ease in grade.
The handicapper has been in command since his runaway win at Leicester (6f, good) in April; today's return to sprinting is in his favour but he's likely to be vulnerable at this level.
Thrown in on last season's best; yet to get going for David O'Meara this time around but he has dropped 10lb for his troubles and the addition of a visor could help; stable also runs Yes I'm Mali.
No problem with fast turf but he has a much better strike-rate on AW; midfield finish at Doncaster last month didn't suggest he is the answer here.

String of good runs since returning in May from a 167-day absence, latterly keeping on for second at Redcar (5f, good) three weeks ago; steps back up to 6f for the first time this year and it could suit.

Hold-up sprinter who ended a near two-year losing run at Pontefract (6f, good) last month; better form when second there three weeks ago; has plenty of C&D form in big fields and he has each-way claims.

OwnerName: S Thompson, Mrs R Carr & G Shields
Spotlight: Picking up his share of prize money this season, making the frame five times either side of his Pontefract (6f, good) win last month; despite that he is still 5lb lower than when a close fourth in this race 12 months ago; each-way shout.
He has been threatening to add to his six wins, with a third at Pontefract and second at Thirsk on his last two starts; on the downside he has been unplaced in all seven visits to York.

Looked as good as ever when second of nine at Catterick (6f, good) on his reappearance in April but that hasn't been a springboard to better things; conditions no problem and he's on a manageable mark but this is a warm race in which to try to bounce back.
Won two 6f events (good/good to soft) for Michael Dods as a 2yo; drew a blank in 2025; makes stable debut in a competitive race after 259 days off; likely best watched.
Won this race in 2022 as a 5yo; no sign of his enthusiasm waning this year, winning three 6f handicaps, and stepping back up in  trip will suit after two good 5f efforts last month; chance.
7f winner at Redcar last November; two solid efforts (6f/7f, good ground) last month but he will need more if he is to defy this mark.

RaceName: Bootleg Beatles Live At Chester Apprentice Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Won a small-field maiden last August and he went close in 1m4f handicaps in his final two runs for Ralph Beckett; left that yard for 90,000gns last autumn; struggled on stable debut here (2m, good) in May but he was a fair fourth over the same C&D last month; has possibilities if he can build on that and he's in the mix back in trip.

RaceName: Bootleg Beatles Live At Chester Apprentice Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: No wins since an odds-on debut success for Karl Burke back in 2023; well handicapped on his old form but he's been well held in his last three runs including over 1m6f at York (good) two weeks ago; down the list.

RaceName: Bootleg Beatles Live At Chester Apprentice Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: All five wins (1m4f-1m6f) have been on AW, but has made the frame here on both starts for current stable, more recently when a staying-on third on his reappearance (2m, good) in May; should be sharper this time and he's respected back in trip.

RaceName: Bootleg Beatles Live At Chester Apprentice Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Won over 2m (good) at this track in June but he's been well held in three subsequent runs including over an extended 1m4f here last time; now goes back up in trip but he's still 3lb higher than for that last success and others are more convincing.

RaceName: Bootleg Beatles Live At Chester Apprentice Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Has a patchy profile but he hasn't been with current yard for long and he won at Bath (1m6f, good) last month; only sixth of seven here (2m)  last time but he ran well for a long way and was beaten less than 4l; still looks on a workable mark and he could make a bold bid back in trip.

RaceName: Bootleg Beatles Live At Chester Apprentice Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Dual-purpose performer but his last Flat win was in December 2023; some creditable efforts off a falling mark on AW (2m) and turf (1m6f) this season but he was tailed off at Newbury last time and needs to bounce back after that; cheekpieces are removed and visor returns.

RaceName: Bootleg Beatles Live At Chester Apprentice Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
OwnerName: Middleham Park Racing Xxiii & M Hulin
Spotlight: Low-mileage 3yo who has won two handicaps (1m4f/1m6f) on turf this season but his form nosedived at Carlisle 17 days ago; still unexposed at this trip but he comes with risks attached after that heavy defeat.

RaceName: Bootleg Beatles Live At Chester Apprentice Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Second start was a five-runner maiden win at Ripon (1m2f, good) in April but she's been held in two handicaps since; still unexposed but she needs to find more to defy this mark and not sure this new trip will suit on pedigree.

RaceName: Simon & Nerys Dutfield Memorial British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Well supported and finished the race strongly when winning here a month ago (6f, soft); different ground and he carries a penalty but the extra furlong seems sure to suit and he has potential.

RaceName: Simon & Nerys Dutfield Memorial British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 150,000gns breeze-up 2yo; among the favourites on debut last month at Pontefract (6f, good) but looked to need the experience, lugging left before sticking on (race has not worked out); will have learned from that and should appreciate the extra furlong.

RaceName: Simon & Nerys Dutfield Memorial British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 325,000gns yearling whose yard got on the board with 2yos for the season on Thursday (poor recent record with them here); no more than a fair debut at Yarmouth in May (7f, good to firm) and needs to improve on that.

RaceName: Simon & Nerys Dutfield Memorial British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 30,000gns yearling who's a half-brother to three useful winners in Ireland (including two for Johnny Murtagh who improved as 3yos); no surprise were he to want more time for a yard struggling for winners of late.

RaceName: Simon & Nerys Dutfield Memorial British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Already gelded late-April foal whose yard hasn't hit its usual high summer form as yet and is 0-9 with 2yos this season (3-42 with them here down the years); the market will be the best guide.

RaceName: Simon & Nerys Dutfield Memorial British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Debut third might have worked out better but he improved on that last time, upped to a stiff 7f, when coming away with a long odds-on winner at Newmarket; sets the standard on that, especially in receipt of 4lb from Undiscovered.
Gained a second win of 2026 at Carlisle (5f) in May but he's been well below par over C&D and at Newcastle since; needs to take a big step forward.

OwnerName: D P Van Der Hoeven & D G Pryde
Spotlight: Completed a hat-trick at Ayr (5f) last summer; built on earlier promise this season when a very good third of 12 at Newcastle (5f) 15 days ago; big shout off a 2lb lower mark here.
Scored five times (including over C&D) in 2025; in very good nick lately too, second for the third consecutive start in 6f handicap here last month; a likely player once more off an unchanged mark.
Resumed winning ways at Pontefract (5f) in June and posted a good second of five over C&D 16 days ago; this C&D winner can go well again nudged up 1lb.
Ended a losing run over 6f here in June and not disgraced when a staying-on third of five over C&D last time; not ruled out if getting a strong gallop to aim at.
A dual 5f scorer in 2025; right back on track of late when placed in handicaps (around 5f) at Wetherby and Ayr so she ought to be thereabouts nudged up 1lb.
It's now 13 starts since he scored over C&D last July and he's largely been below par this season, seventh of ten at Musselburgh (5f) 11 days ago; others are preferred.
Ex-Irish maiden who has yet to make the frame in five runs for her current yard, a never-dangerous sixth of ten at Musselburgh (5f) 19 days ago; not easy to make a case for.

All four wins over 1m4f, the last three on Tapeta; not seen since a career best to win at Wolverhampton in January and he's 4lb higher here; twice runner-up on turf last year; may need to hit a new turf high but he requires a second look given this small field.
Won under 7lb claimer in four-runner C&D race (good to firm) last July off 1lb higher; Yarmouth second one month ago and should be seriously involved.

Sole win (12 races) came in a 1m2f handicap in April 2025; made a respectable debut for new yard this April but took a backward step next time; she's now 6lb below her peak mark and moves back up from 1m2f, having been below form once each over 1m3f and 1m4f last term; stamina in the family, though.

Acts on good to firm; placed over 1m2f on first four starts this season, before comfortably justifying 4-6 favouritism in a 11.6f novice at Lingfield on latest outing; this unchanged, opening handicap mark seems to demand more but he may have more to offer at this sort of trip.

Often front-runner; soundly beaten at the big Chester and York meetings in May but fairly solid otherwise this term and returned to winning ways in a five-runner race at Thirsk (1m4f, good to firm) ten days ago; back up 3lb but considered seriously in another small field.

Six-time AW winner; scored for the first time on turf in a 6f handicap three starts ago; fair sixth at the same venue on his next start, but in rear in the apprentice race won by today's rival Monastere ten days ago.

Three turf wins include one at Fairyhouse (6f) in 2024; high head-carriage proved his undoing when second off his reduced mark at the same venue on Wednesday; had excuses at the same track last week (upset in stalls, not clear run) when well backed in an apprentice handicap won by Monastere.
Ran her best race so far when fourth at 40-1 over 1m at this venue last month, a head in front of fifth-placed Notforalongtime; finished behind that rival here last time.
Clear highlight of last year's form was a 6f win at Sligo in August; only one pound higher now; lacks the advantage of a recent run.
Prolific AW winner (9-32); off-putting 1-31 record on turf but some good placings this season at Cork and the Curragh; a bit below best over 1m at this venue on his two most recent starts.

Weak maiden form; produced massive improvement when second at 66-1 in a 6f Fairyhouse event last month on handicap debut; despite that encouraging run, he was sent off at 20-1 for an apprentice handicap over the same course and distance and belied his odds by beating Whatswrongnow (first reserve); raised 8lb, rider can use his 5lb claim now; leading chance.

Maiden after 18 starts; failed to build on a bright start to last season; uneven form but can be rated a possible future winner on her 6f Fairyhouse second last month; the winner was second of 17 at the same venue last week.

Showed next to nothing in three 7f AW maidens; made turf debut over 5f at Cork; not disgraced, bearing in mind that his rider had to cope with a slipped saddle; slowly away and had plenty to do before keeping on into sixth over an extended 7f at Roscommon in first handicap; shorter trip may not be ideal.

No win on turf since 2022 but scored at Dundalk (7f) for the third time in February; not at his best at the same venue the following month; better when seventh of 18 at Fairyhouse; down the field at this venue a month ago.

Consistent over 6f on AW during the winter and into April when second to the veteran Jered Maddox; twice runner-up on turf last year; badly drawn at Fairyhouse last month and should not be judged harshly on that run.
Mild promise on only 2024 start for Kevin Coleman; missed last season; in rear in two maidens this term; her chance is not obvious on handicap debut.
No sign of worthwhile form in five maiden outings; fifth of 13 over 7f at this venue on handicap debut was a step in the right direction; suffered slight interference inside the last furlong.

Ten-race maiden; has dropped 21lb in the ratings since initially assessed last season; some encouragement can be taken from her fifth of 21 over 5f at Naas last month; first time with cheekpieces now.

Four-time winner, the latest at Cork last July (6f, good to firm), later a good second at Bellewstown (1m, good to yielding); totally out of form since then; her ability to handle quick ground is a positive.

Blinkers on for her Newmarket debut (7f, good to firm) when coming from last to first in a small field, despite over-racing early; it is hard to know the merits of that as yet but she's entitled to improve for a yard with an unsurprisingly good record here.

Debut fifth in a big field at Newbury (7f, good) in May has been well advertised by the principals since; easy to back that day and is open to improvement, having since been withdrawn on account of soft ground; shortlisted.
Beaten a long way on her soft-ground debut five weeks ago; likely she's one for handicaps later on, probably over a bit further.
Well-related filly who fared best of the newcomers, behind a long odds-on favourite, on her Windsor debut early last month; tonight's extra furlong will suit both on that evidence and on pedigree.

OwnerName: D B Clark & A R Bentall
Spotlight: Trainer, who also saddles March Of Time, won this with a newcomer last season; this 8,000gns foal, who's a half-sister to winners over various distances, will have her anticipated chances reflected in the market.

RaceName: John & Margaret McCluskey Memorial Celebrations EBF Maiden Stakes (Hamilton Park 2yo Qual) (GBB)
Spotlight: Cotai Glory colt who shaped well when a debut second over 6f here but failed to build on it with sixth of 11 in 6f York novice two weeks ago, fading late; remains with potential now dropping to 5f.

RaceName: John & Margaret McCluskey Memorial Celebrations EBF Maiden Stakes (Hamilton Park 2yo Qual) (GBB)
Spotlight: 9-1, made a promising start with second of six in 5f novice at Newcastle two weeks ago, pulling clear of rest; this son of Naval Crown seems sure to build on it; big player.

RaceName: John & Margaret McCluskey Memorial Celebrations EBF Maiden Stakes (Hamilton Park 2yo Qual) (GBB)
Spotlight: 18-1 and green when a debut fourth of five in maiden here (6f, good) last month; this No Nay Never colt can improve a fair bit for the initial experience.

RaceName: John & Margaret McCluskey Memorial Celebrations EBF Maiden Stakes (Hamilton Park 2yo Qual) (GBB)
Spotlight: 12-1 and very green when last of six in novice at Pontefract (6f, good) on his debut 20 days ago; lots more is needed from this son of Minzaal.

RaceName: John & Margaret McCluskey Memorial Celebrations EBF Maiden Stakes (Hamilton Park 2yo Qual) (GBB)
Spotlight: 14-1, hinted at promise when a debut seventh of ten in maiden at Musselburgh (5f, good to soft) in April; since gelded and likely to take a step forward.

RaceName: John & Margaret McCluskey Memorial Celebrations EBF Maiden Stakes (Hamilton Park 2yo Qual) (GBB)
Spotlight: Blue Point filly who has progressed with each of her three runs, posting a good third of eight in 5f Catterick maiden 19 days ago; she may do better still so is not out of things.

RaceName: DAR Golf And Mann Family Handicap
Spotlight: Not without a chance on her best juvenile form, namely a third placing in a 7f Down Royal nursery; saddle slipped on her reappearance at Cork.

RaceName: DAR Golf And Mann Family Handicap
Spotlight: Won an early-season 5f Cork maiden last year; did not appear until March this year; two Dundalk runs leave her with questions to answer.

RaceName: DAR Golf And Mann Family Handicap
Spotlight: All four runs at Dundalk; fair fourth of 13 at 200-1 in a 7f maiden in February; never a threat in a handicap later that month.

RaceName: DAR Golf And Mann Family Handicap
Spotlight: Showed improved form to take second over 5f at Down Royal in May, just denied after making most of the running; failed to replicate that form over the same course and distance last time; worth considering with new headgear combination and champion jockey aboard.

RaceName: DAR Golf And Mann Family Handicap
Spotlight: Dundalk win in January (1m) seems to have been a flash in the pan; turf runs offer little encouragement; cheekpieces added to tongue-tie worn last time.

RaceName: DAR Golf And Mann Family Handicap
Spotlight: 6f maiden winner at Dundalk in February; pleasing run when third over 6f at Fairyhouse early last month; struggled from a poor draw at the same venue a week later.

RaceName: DAR Golf And Mann Family Handicap
Spotlight: Showed some ability on AW over 5f-6f during the winter; has failed to beat a single rival in two turf starts since being gelded; can be ruled out.

RaceName: DAR Golf And Mann Family Handicap
Spotlight: Her fifth on handicap debut at Gowran (1m, good to yielding) looked something to build on; two subsequent runs have been disappointing; cheekpieces tried now.

RaceName: DAR Golf And Mann Family Handicap
Spotlight: Set some very stiff tasks in 6f juvenile maidens won by high-profile Ballydoyle-trained horses, Albert Einstein, Gstaad and Pierre Bonnard; poor nursery run at Bellewstown, where soft ground may have been an issue; has been gelded, premature to dismiss.

RaceName: DAR Golf And Mann Family Handicap
Spotlight: Last three races have been over 6f at Fairyhouse, placed in two of them; much improved when second in a 17-runner contest in which Jampa Ling was third; failed to reproduce that form a week later; bounced back to take third behind Monastere ten days ago; the winner is due to put that form to the test in the 6.00 race.

RaceName: DAR Golf And Mann Family Handicap
Spotlight: First time in the frame when fourth at Roscommon in May; in rear at that venue on latest; plenty to prove against that background.

RaceName: DAR Golf And Mann Family Handicap
Spotlight: Lightly raced and in good hands, but will need to show massive improvement in first-time blinkers.

RaceName: DAR Golf And Mann Family Handicap
Spotlight: Did most of her racing for her previous trainer at Dundalk; modest form overall; up against it on stable debut.

RaceName: DAR Golf And Mann Family Handicap
Spotlight: Not a bad run over 7f at Down Royal in May; appeared not to get the longer trip at Ballinrobe last time; others have stronger claims.

Front-runner who's well suited by a stiff 1m2f, winning twice last summer and going close over C&D in the autumn off tonight's mark; soon gave way once headed on her recent AW comeback from eight months off and needs to improve a lot on that.

Front-runner who won her first five handicaps early last summer, latterly off 8lb lower, climbing more than two stone up the weights; maybe good to soft ground was behind a tame comeback in the spring but she may need help from the handicapper.

OwnerName: HM The King & HM The Queen
Spotlight: Pulled hard when beaten at odds-on on her 7f comeback; it's not hard to pick holes in her Nottingham novice win, upped to this trip 16 days ago, but she did it in decent style and is unexposed entering handicap company; still holds an entry in next weekend's Irish Oaks.

Second off this mark to a useful prospect last time and that was better, having run Flat at Chester and then over-raced and not seen out 1m4f at York; had plenty of racing this last year and this, however, and remains vulnerable to improvers.

Windsor fourth has been well advertised since (first three all successful next time) and she did her bit when winning a 1m2f Lingfield handicap last month; that continued the pattern of steady progress and she may well be up to defying a 5lb rise.

420,000gns yearling who faced a useful winner when well held at odds-on on Tapeta in the spring; dam won over this trip and has produced a 1m2f winner, so the longer trip ought to help now she's handicapping; also wears a first-time tongue-tie; still has some potential.

RaceName: Rocca Restaurant & Ora Bar Hamilton Handicap
Spotlight: Ended a long losing run when cosily making all in five-runner handicap at Nottingham (6f) three weeks ago; up 2lb but not taken lightly in his current mood.

RaceName: Rocca Restaurant & Ora Bar Hamilton Handicap
Spotlight: Gained a breakthrough success on his yard debut (formerly with Ivan Furtado) in 7.5f handicap at Chester last month; needs to back it up dropped to 6f here off a 4lb higher mark.

RaceName: Rocca Restaurant & Ora Bar Hamilton Handicap
Spotlight: A fair 5.5f winner as a 2yo but she offered little in three runs last summer; needs to take a major step forward after 11 months off the track.

RaceName: Rocca Restaurant & Ora Bar Hamilton Handicap
Spotlight: Gained a first win for his current yard at Ayr (7f) in May and has continued in good nick, fourth of 11 back there five days ago; drops back in trip here but he's well in the mix again.

RaceName: Rocca Restaurant & Ora Bar Hamilton Handicap
Spotlight: Bagged a remarkable 11th C&D success in June; only sixth of eight in 7f Musselburgh handicap 11 days ago but no surprise to see her bounce back here.
Homebred gelding is by the Kodiac stallion Mr Kodilyne; half-brother to 2m hurdle winner; dam dual bumper winner, out of Swedish 1m winning half-sister to 2,000 Guineas runner-up Even Top.

Smart level of form in three runs at the Curragh last year, albeit RPRs suggest a three-figure mark flatters him; competed against the likes of Benvenuto Cellini and Hawk Mountain, including when third of five in Group 2 Beresford Stakes; tongue-tie was reached for when uneasy Evens fav for Fairyhouse return over 7f, and ran a shocker; that raises a note of caution, but what's going to beat him?

Homebred Holy Roman Emperor gelding; dam unraced half-sister to 1m2f/11.6f Listed winner Flying Fairies, out of unraced sister to 1m2f Group 3 winner Meath; 75-rated half-brother Tokyo Treasure runs earlier on the card; 40s for debut over 1m at Naas, never involved and beaten 10l.
Awtaad newcomer is a half-brother to 2m4f/3m1f hurdle winner Cornmarket; dam placed over 5f/6f at two, half-sister to 7f/1m Group 2 winner Safe Voyage.
Invincible Army newcomer representing rookie trainer; half-sister to 6f winner Ran Amok (6f RPR 82) and Best Law (9.4f-1m4f; 80).
Homebred daughter of Elzaam; never a factor in soft-ground maiden over 7f here last month, beaten 22l.

OwnerName: The 19th Hole Syndicate & J S Moore
Spotlight: Consistent record on AW features two wins; has failed to transfer that form back to turf in last two starts, taking record in this sphere to 0-7; something to prove.

Absent since a comfortable novice win at Lingfield (kept to 1m on AW) in December; open to further progress now handicapping and upped in trip; interesting on reappearance, especially if strong in the betting.
Has a solid record in handicaps, completing an AW double then making the frame twice on turf; however, has shown no further improvement for the switch to this sphere.

Thrice-raced filly who finished only a modest fourth on soft going most recently; has a useful pedigree, represents major yard and may well improve returned to this faster ground on handicap debut; respected.

Comfortably on top in 0-55 handicap at Bath (1m2f, good) last time, opening her account at the fifth attempt; may build on that win and show further progress; receives weight all round upped in grade.

A fairly useful 1m4f winner for Ralph Beckett in 2025; has steadily found his form for his current yard this season, posting a good third of eight in 1m2f handicap at Doncaster eight days ago; firmly in the picture stepping back up in trip.
A four-time winner (at up to 1m5f) in 2025; easily best run this season when a clear second of six in 1m4f handicap at Ripon 24 days ago; a likely player despite taking a 2lb rise here.
Scored at Ayr (1m2f) last April in a light campaign in 2025; back from 12 months off with an encouraging Ripon fifth but beat only one at Chester (1m4f) since; needs to bounce back.
Gained a second success of the season on her first go over 1m4f in nine-runner York handicap four weeks ago; up 2lb but she must enter calculations if her stamina holds out over this longer trip.

A dual winner (at up to 1m2f) last season; recorded a very good second over C&D in June and didn't enjoy a smooth run when fifth of 12 at Newcastle (1m4f) two weeks ago; remains one to be interested in.
A fair 1m winner for James Barrett in Ireland; off 13 months before coming in last of eight for her new yard in 7f Musselburgh handicap 11 days ago; others are much preferred.

RaceName: Conference & Banqueting Facilities @ Limerick Median Auction Fillies Maiden (Median Auction Race)
Spotlight: From a top Moyglare family that includes Sapphire and Kyprios; okay run in Listed race at Naas on sole start at two; 12s, never involved in 1m1f Curragh maiden on testing ground in April, but bare form is not bad in the context of what will be needed here; yard in fine form.

RaceName: Conference & Banqueting Facilities @ Limerick Median Auction Fillies Maiden (Median Auction Race)
OwnerName: Carl Anthony Howell & Mrs Pauline Condon
Spotlight: By Lucky Vega and cost E30,000 as a yearling; second foal of 1m4f AW winner (RPR 98) who is a sister to useful 1m2f/1m4f winner Springbank, out of dual Group 3 7f winner Lady Springbank; tongue-tie.

RaceName: Conference & Banqueting Facilities @ Limerick Median Auction Fillies Maiden (Median Auction Race)
OwnerName: Mohammed Bin Hamad Khalifa Al Attiyah
Spotlight: By Australia and her most notable sibling is Group 1 sprint winner Librisa Breeze, but she's out of a 1m2f winner and is also closely related to one; 12-1, missed the break when fifth on soft ground at Leopardstown, 5l behind Stellar Moment; stable jockey has chosen to ride stablemate.

RaceName: Conference & Banqueting Facilities @ Limerick Median Auction Fillies Maiden (Median Auction Race)
Spotlight: All runs have been on easy ground, best of them a 5l second to an odds-on chance over 10.5f at Cork in May; faces quicker ground and longer trip now.

RaceName: Conference & Banqueting Facilities @ Limerick Median Auction Fillies Maiden (Median Auction Race)
Spotlight: Homebred by Sottsass; dam 1m AW Listed winner, half-sister to Gallinule Stakes winner Crossfirehurricane, family of French 1,000 Guineas winner Flotilla; 11s for debut over 1m at Gowran, was getting the message late on when beaten 3.5l into fourth; form hasn't worked out that well, but she has potential.

RaceName: Conference & Banqueting Facilities @ Limerick Median Auction Fillies Maiden (Median Auction Race)
Spotlight: Crystal Ocean half-sister to yard's winners Lamprog (7f/1m1f) and Newfire (1m); dam a half-sister to Listed-placed 7f-1m2f winner Thunderspeed; probably best watched.

RaceName: Conference & Banqueting Facilities @ Limerick Median Auction Fillies Maiden (Median Auction Race)
Spotlight: Well beaten over 7f at the Curragh in March; after three months off, again well held over 7.5f at Roscommon; half-sister to pair of 7f winners, but at least dam's sister won over this trip.

RaceName: Conference & Banqueting Facilities @ Limerick Median Auction Fillies Maiden (Median Auction Race)
Spotlight: Big prices for two runs over 1m2f on soft ground; much improved at Leopardstown last month when a keeping-on third but not sure how strong that form is; by Ulysses, and Kris Kin features in her pedigree, so grounds for optimism that she'll stay this far.

RaceName: Conference & Banqueting Facilities @ Limerick Median Auction Fillies Maiden (Median Auction Race)
Spotlight: Both runs have been in this headgear and over this trip; AW debut was okay, 66-1 yard second-string when well beaten at Fairyhouse lately.

Expensive to follow in her three starts as a 2yo but she returned as the form choice in an eight-runner fillies' novice at Ripon (6f, good) in May and won by 7l at 4-5 in first-time cheekpieces, going on over 1f out; that was an improved effort and she goes handicapping off a realistic mark.

Won a 5f maiden at Catterick last August but he's 0-6 in handicaps this year and has generally underperformed; second in first-time headgear off this reduced mark at Kempton (6f, AW) on latest outing but markedly better is needed, even in this small field.

Close call on debut (5f) last May was easily best effort until he won a seven-runner handicap at Nottingham (6f) last Saturday, coming from last to challenge over 1f out; they were his only two races on good to firm; tongue tied last two starts; back up 3lb but has a shout in this small field.

RaceName: Get Your Tickets For Cube Glass Ladies Night Handicap
Spotlight: A fair 7f winner for John and Thady Gosden in 2025; back on song for his current yard with a clear second of seven in 1m Pontefract handicap 12 days ago; a likely player nudged up 2lb.

RaceName: Get Your Tickets For Cube Glass Ladies Night Handicap
Spotlight: Ended a long losing sequence (on his yard debut) over C&D in May but comes here on the back of a below-form sixth at Newcastle (1m) last month; needs to get back on track.

RaceName: Get Your Tickets For Cube Glass Ladies Night Handicap
Spotlight: Shaped well when runner-up in 1m novices at Nottingham on his first two runs but failed to land the odds when third of seven in 1m Leicester maiden four weeks ago; since gelded and this son of Lucky Vega remains with potential on his handicap debut.

RaceName: Get Your Tickets For Cube Glass Ladies Night Handicap
Spotlight: Fair ex-German 1m1f winner; got off the mark for his current yard over C&D in May but beat only one at Ayr (1m2f) on soft ground since; no surprise if he bounced back here.

RaceName: Get Your Tickets For Cube Glass Ladies Night Handicap
Spotlight: Runner-up on his first three starts in 1m AW maidens but he's failed to kick on sent handicapping, only 12th at Kempton (1m) 12 days ago; has a bit to prove.

RaceName: Panoramic Restaurant At Limerick Racecourse Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Winning hurdler for Noel Meade; regressive on Flat since showing winning potential in 2024; rated a respectable 79 at one stage, now down to 60.

RaceName: Panoramic Restaurant At Limerick Racecourse Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Has done plenty of racing since a C&D win last summer; generally modest form this season, with the exception of a Navan third on his penultimate start.

RaceName: Panoramic Restaurant At Limerick Racecourse Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Out of form before a Dundalk win in May, down the field behind Deluca Chop at Down Royal; plenty to find on that evidence.

RaceName: Panoramic Restaurant At Limerick Racecourse Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Has made the frame in four of her six races this term; needs to find her form again after two less encouraging runs; tries a new trip.

RaceName: Panoramic Restaurant At Limerick Racecourse Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Failed to beat a single rival in two runs at two; missed last season; never in serious contention in a 1m7f maiden at Leopardstown.

RaceName: Panoramic Restaurant At Limerick Racecourse Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: In the frame twice over hurdles at Kilbeggan and Downpatrick since Flat win at Sligo; up 4lb for that, same 7lb claimer aboard; chance has to be take seriously.

RaceName: Panoramic Restaurant At Limerick Racecourse Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Sole Flat win came on AW at Wolverhampton in December 2024 over an extended 1m1f; one of his better turf efforts when sixth at Fairyhouse last month; soundly beaten in third when favourite at Tramore on Tuesday, the winner was well handicapped.

RaceName: Panoramic Restaurant At Limerick Racecourse Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Had shown little, Flat or hurdles, before running way above expectations (300-1) in conditions hurdle at Ballinrobe; another fair run in a Downpatrick maiden hurdle last time.

RaceName: Panoramic Restaurant At Limerick Racecourse Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Consistent in defeat on AW over winter/spring, placing in four consecutive runs at 1m4f-2m; continued that form back on turf at Roscommon in May when again runner-up; deserved his change of fortune when scoring at Navan; backed that up at Down Royal; ground-versatile and hat-trick not out of the question.

RaceName: Panoramic Restaurant At Limerick Racecourse Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Modest but progressive AW handicapper last winter, winning over 1m2f and placed at 1m4f; good chance now based on last week's pleasing Bellewstown second which came after a break.

RaceName: Panoramic Restaurant At Limerick Racecourse Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Placed twice in low-grade handicaps last season and fourth in a Ballinrobe handicap four starts ago; ran in a maiden last time; each-way chance if recovering last year's best form.

RaceName: Panoramic Restaurant At Limerick Racecourse Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Moderate fourth in a C&D maiden last month; one of the less exposed runners; could feature at this modest level on handicap debut.

RaceName: Panoramic Restaurant At Limerick Racecourse Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Won a Newbury handicap for Sir Michael Stoute in 2024 off 81 (1m2f, good); has come down in the world; poor on AW and modest on turf this year.

RaceName: Panoramic Restaurant At Limerick Racecourse Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Best effort so far when sixth over 7f at Cork in May; contrasting ground conditions now and needs to find more.

RaceName: Hampton By Hilton Hamilton Park Handicap
Spotlight: Enjoyed a remarkable 2025 when a six-time winner, including over C&D; below par this season until third of seven in 1m1f handicap at Musselburgh 11 days ago but very much one to consider off a reduced mark now.

RaceName: Hampton By Hilton Hamilton Park Handicap
Spotlight: Scored five times last season (twice over C&D) but yet to hit top form this term, just a fair fifth of nine to Daring Leader at Pontefract (1m) 12 days ago; needs to take a step forward.

RaceName: Hampton By Hilton Hamilton Park Handicap
Spotlight: Modest maiden who wasn't discredited in first-time cheekpieces when fifth of nine in 1m Carlisle handicap last month; needs to build on it if she's to gain a first win at the seventh attempt.

RaceName: Hampton By Hilton Hamilton Park Handicap
Spotlight: Yet to score and he's failed to beat a rival in 7f handicaps at Ayr and Musselburgh this season; easy to look elsewhere.

RaceName: Hampton By Hilton Hamilton Park Handicap
Spotlight: Saxon Warrior filly who took a step forward for new yard (formerly with George Boughey) when a neck second of seven in 8.5f handicap at Beverley eight days ago; a player nudged up 1lb.

RaceName: Hampton By Hilton Hamilton Park Handicap
Spotlight: Fair juvenile maiden; offered little this season until much better signs when fourth of seven (Amber Hamur in second) in 8.5f Beverley handicap eight days ago, staying on well late after a tardy start; must enter calculations eased 1lb.

RaceName: Private Suites At Limerick Racecourse Handicap
Spotlight: Did hold an Irish Oaks entry but had shown little over 7f/1m until ready winner of a restricted C&D maiden on soft ground last month; bred for this trip, saw it out well and would probably stay farther if asked; up 9lb and faster ground to contend with, but she's probably just getting going now.

RaceName: Private Suites At Limerick Racecourse Handicap
Spotlight: Handicap debutant whose middle maiden run, over this trip at Dundalk, was decent; trainer's course record on the Flat (6-11 last four seasons, and 3-3 this year) is certainly a positive and looks like one that could be competitive now given a mark.

RaceName: Private Suites At Limerick Racecourse Handicap
Spotlight: His first maiden run, fifth over 1m2f at Leopardstown, was his best; 11s for the 1m4f Apprentice Derby at the Curragh, didn't settle effectively in front and faded in the last quarter-mile.

RaceName: Private Suites At Limerick Racecourse Handicap
Spotlight: Fair runs in maidens before stepping up in trip for handicap debut at Down Royal, beaten 8l when third over 1m2f; hard to know if this trip will suit, but there are middle-distance winners on dam's side of pedigree; gets fast ground for the first time here.

RaceName: Private Suites At Limerick Racecourse Handicap
Spotlight: Improved for having tongue tied in recent restricted-maiden outings; went close over 9.5f at Ballinrobe and third to subsequent wide-margin handicap winner Teologia over 10.5f (good) at Down Royal; holds Hassaniya on collateral form with the latter; first handicap.

RaceName: Private Suites At Limerick Racecourse Handicap
Spotlight: Mildly promising runs in two maidens and a Curragh handicap in the spring (7f/1m); maybe soft ground was the reason she bombed over 1m1f at Leopardstown last month; tongue-tie now fitted and stamina to prove over this far.
A four-time C&D winner; yet to hit top form this season but took a step back in right direction with sixth of ten in 1m2f Ayr handicap 22 days ago; very much one to consider off an easing mark.
Scored four times (at up to 1m4f) in 2025; back in good form of late, runner-up in 1m4f handicap at Newcastle two weeks ago; well in the mix nudged up 1lb.

A fair 8.5f winner for David Loughnane in 2025; little show first two runs for new yard but much better signs (in change of headgear) with third of ten in 1m1f handicap at Carlisle last month; not out of things on his first go over this trip.
Fair 1m juvenile maiden; off nine months/gelded before posting a solid fifth of ten in 1m1f handicap at Carlisle last month, giving the impression he's worth a try over a longer trip; considered.

Time Test filly who advanced her form on her handicap debut when a staying-on sixth of ten in 1m1f handicap at Carlisle last month; this longer distance should suit so she merits serious consideration with better to come after just four runs.
Resumed winning ways over 1m1f here in June but only fifth of six in 1m1f Musselburgh handicap 19 days ago; needs to bounce back.

RaceName: Bowling Green Stakes presented by Emerald Evocations (Grade 2) (Turf)
Spotlight: Career wins-runs record of 7-20 and he was in the money in a stakes last August; close up second at Churchill Downs when dropped to 1m 1/2f 51 days ago in an allowance optional claimer; leading contender; absence is no concern as has previously gone well off a break and he can take this step up in class in his stride.

RaceName: Bowling Green Stakes presented by Emerald Evocations (Grade 2) (Turf)
OwnerName: Jacques Dupuis Jr, Luke Bourque & Bobby Sutton
Spotlight: Successful twice in his last five starts; displayed a fair level of form when 3 1/2l runner-up in an allowance listed at Delaware Park four weeks ago; more on his plate but not dismissed.

RaceName: Bowling Green Stakes presented by Emerald Evocations (Grade 2) (Turf)
OwnerName: Stonelea Stable LLC & Bonnie Rye Stable
Spotlight: Admirable career strike-rate (four from 19 to date); recent efforts have generally been solid; close up behind Soleil Volant when fourth 28 days ago beaten just over 4l at Delaware Park in an allowance listed; faces a tougher assignment this time so he can be taken on.

RaceName: Bowling Green Stakes presented by Emerald Evocations (Grade 2) (Turf)
Spotlight: Steadily progressive sort who has taken well to Pattern company in best effort when second to prolific scorer Rebel's Romance in the Group 2 City Of Gold (1m4f) at Meydan in February having previously won the Group 3 Dubail Millennium (1m2f); not disgraced when sixth to Ombudsmen in Group 1 Dubai Turf (1m2f); handlwes quick ground and a major player here.

RaceName: Bowling Green Stakes presented by Emerald Evocations (Grade 2) (Turf)
Spotlight: Finished fourth, beaten a fair way at Belmont At The Big A upped to 1m1 1/2f in May in an allowance optional claimer; useful at this level; possesses winning form at this track; tackling an extra 1 1/2f now.

RaceName: Bowling Green Stakes presented by Emerald Evocations (Grade 2) (Turf)
Spotlight: Not beaten far in an allowance Grade 3 at Churchill Downs six weeks ago; has been consistent of late and has made an impressive start to his career with three wins from six starts; up in class but ought to be on the premises.

RaceName: Bowling Green Stakes presented by Emerald Evocations (Grade 2) (Turf)
Spotlight: Creditable effort to finish third in an allowance race at Keeneland upped to 1m4f three months ago; consistent lately but wins achieved at a lower level; Manuel Franco booked; with more on his plate others make more appeal.
